title: Change Feed Polling Endpoint

story.i_want_to: poll for all state changes since a given timestamp, filterable by project, entity type, and action
story.so_that: I can detect what changed since my last poll cycle and react accordingly without maintaining server-side cursors or WebSocket connections

rationale: Orchestrators need a lightweight, stateless mechanism to discover changes across the entire project. Polling via timestamp is the simplest approach that supports crash recovery — the orchestrator just remembers its last poll timestamp and asks for everything since then. This avoids the complexity of server-side cursor management or WebSocket infrastructure while still enabling near-real-time coordination.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/changes?since=<ISO8601> returns all audit log entries with inserted_at > since timestamp for the authenticated tenant, ordered by inserted_at ascending.
The since parameter is required. Requests without it return 400 Bad Request with a descriptive error message.
The endpoint supports optional project_id filter that scopes results to entities belonging to that project (stories, epics, and their related audit entries).
The endpoint supports optional entity_type filter (e.g., story, epic, project, agent) to narrow results to specific entity types.
The endpoint supports optional action filter (e.g., status_changed, created, verified, rejected) to narrow results to specific actions.

Results are capped at a configurable maximum (default 1000) per request. If more results exist, the response includes a has_more boolean flag and the last entry's inserted_at as next_since for the next poll.
The endpoint is accessible to agent role and above (agent, orchestrator, user). All roles can poll the change feed.
Results are tenant-scoped via RLS. A tenant can only see changes for their own entities.

The response includes each entry's id, entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_type, actor_label, new_state, metadata, and inserted_at. The old_state field is omitted from the change feed response to reduce payload size (available via full audit endpoint).
Multiple filters can be combined (e.g., since + project_id + entity_type + action).

test_cases.name: basic polling returns changes since timestamp
preconditions: 3 audit log entries created at known timestamps (t1, t2, t3 where t1 < t2 < t3)
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Returns entries at t2 and t3 (entries after t1)
Entries are ordered by inserted_at ascending
Each entry includes entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_label, new_state, inserted_at

test_cases.name: missing since parameter returns 400
expected_results: Response is 400 Bad Request
Error message indicates since parameter is required

test_cases.name: project_id filter scopes results correctly
preconditions: Audit entries exist for entities in both projects
expected_results: Response includes only changes related to Project A entities
No changes from Project B appear in the results

test_cases.name: entity_type and action filters work together
preconditions: Audit entries with various entity_types (story, epic, project) and actions (created, updated, status_changed)
expected_results: Response includes only entries where entity_type='story' AND action='status_changed'
Entries with other entity_types or actions are excluded

test_cases.name: result cap and has_more flag
preconditions: More than 1000 audit log entries for the tenant (or override cap to smaller value for test)
steps: GET /api/v1/changes?since=<early_timestamp> (with result cap set to 10 for testing)
expected_results: Response includes exactly 10 entries (the cap)
has_more is true
next_since contains the inserted_at of the last returned entry
Using next_since in a subsequent poll returns the remaining entries

test_cases.name: tenant isolation on change feed
preconditions: Audit entries exist for both tenants
steps: GET /api/v1/changes?since=<past_timestamp> with Tenant A's API key
expected_results: Response includes only Tenant A's changes
No entries from Tenant B appear

test_cases.name: agent role can access change feed
preconditions: Audit entries exist for the tenant
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Returns change entries for the tenant

test_cases.name: invalid since timestamp returns 400
expected_results: Both requests return 400 Bad Request
Error message indicates invalid timestamp format

technical_notes: Reuses the audit_log table from US-3.1 — no new tables needed. This is a read-only endpoint over existing data.
Controller: ChangeController with index action. Route: GET /api/v1/changes.

The project_id filter requires a join through entity relationships — stories belong to epics which belong to projects. Store project_id in audit_log metadata during logging for efficient filtering, or join through entity tables.
Consider adding a project_id column to audit_log (denormalized) for efficient project-scoped change feed queries. Alternative: store project_id in the metadata jsonb.
Result cap (default 1000) should be configurable via application config. Response shape: {data: [...], has_more: bool, next_since: ISO8601|null}.
Omit old_state from the change feed response to reduce payload size. Clients can use GET /audit for full state diffs.
Index on (tenant_id, inserted_at) from US-3.1 supports the primary query pattern (since > timestamp, scoped by tenant).
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
